factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed results in its pricing power. While there is a slight increase in yield, passenger revenue is declining due to reduced demand, and challenges remain from significant ticket cancellations and waived change fees.

evidence.text: Decreased revenue year-over-year is primarily due to the near complete loss of demand due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The loss of change fee revenue, combined with ongoing significant ticket cancellation activity, has adversely impacted our revenues and liquidity.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining significantly across the company, driven by reduced travel demand resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic. The declines are evident in passenger counts, which were approximately 5% of prior year levels, although there were slight improvements with leisure travelers contributing to daily peaks around 20% of prior year levels. Additionally, a 40% decrease in capacity and a 21 point decrease in load factor further indicate the severity of the volume reduction.

evidence.text: Passenger counts were approximately 5% of prior year levels. Throughout the quarter, we began to see slight improvements in demand and new bookings, with daily passenger counts peaking around 20% of prior year levels, primarily driven by leisure travelers.
A 40% decrease in capacity and a 21 point decrease in load factor led to reduced passenger revenue.
The global pandemic caused by COVID-19... has resulted in an unprecedented decline in business and leisure travel.

factors.summary: Overall demand for air travel has significantly decreased due to the COVID-19 pandemic, with notable impacts from the cancellation of large public events, suspension of business travel, and implementation of stay-at-home orders. The pandemic has presented unprecedented challenges, driving demand to historic lows and resulting in decreased departures and passengers.

evidence.text: The impacts of COVID-19 on our business have been unprecedented, and have presented us with some of the greatest challenges in our 88-year history. The cancellation of large public events, suspension of business travel, closure of popular tourist destinations and implementation of stay-at-home orders throughout the country beginning in March 2020, and continuing through to June 2020, has driven demand for air travel to historic lows.
Decreased departures and passengers as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic.
The global pandemic caused by COVID-19... has resulted in an unprecedented decline in business and leisure travel.

factors.summary: Margins are under significant pressure, primarily due to reduced demand and increased costs. Despite some cost reductions, the decline in revenues has outpaced the decrease in expenses, leading to compression in operating margins.

evidence.text: Total operating expenses decreased $1.2 billion, or 63%, compared to the second quarter of 2019. [...] The shift to pretax loss was driven primarily by a decrease in operating revenues of $1.9 billion stemming from the sharp decline in demand and $69 million in special charges from asset impairment, offset by a decrease in non-fuel operating expenses of $772 million, including wage offsets from the payroll support program of the CARES Act of $362 million. Pretax loss was also offset by a decrease in fuel expense of $443 million.
Total operating expenses decreased $1.1 billion, or 29%, compared to the first six months of 2019, but revenues decreased more significantly.
These measures, while helpful in slowing the rate at which we utilize our cash, are not expected to fully recover the loss of cash as a result of decreased ticket sales.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is decreasing across the company, driven by reduced flying operations and parked aircraft due to the pandemic. The company is adjusting its capacity utilization in response to lower demand.

evidence.text: Aircraft utilization 5.6 11.1 (49.5)% 8.8 10.7 (17.8)%
Reduced departures and capacity reductions as a result of the pandemic
The company has implemented and continues to implement a comprehensive strategy to mitigate the impacts on our business

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a significant decline in load factors and a decrease in RASM, driven by reduced demand and capacity adjustments. These declines are contributing to lower passenger revenue.

evidence.text: 40% decrease in capacity and a 21 point decrease in load factor led to reduced passenger revenue.
The global pandemic caused by COVID-19... has resulted in an unprecedented decline in business and leisure travel.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing favorable fuel costs due to decreased prices, but its hedging positions have resulted in losses and overhedging relative to its target, which could lead to further cash losses.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense decreased $443 million, or 88%, compared to the second quarter of 2019. [...] Raw fuel expense per gallon for the three months ended June 30, 2020 decreased by approximately 51% due to lower West Coast jet fuel prices. [...] The company's economic fuel cost per gallon was $1.20 compared to $2.27 in the prior year.
Losses on settled hedges were $10 million in 2020 compared to $7 million in 2019.
Given the sharp decline in demand and our capacity resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic, we are currently overhedged relative to our target of 50% of consumption through the remainder of 2020.

factors.summary: The company is evaluating its order backlogs and fleet strategy, with potential deferrals due to reduced demand. However, there is no specific information provided on backlog orders or book-to-bill ratios in other sections of the filing.

evidence.text: No information on backlog orders or book-to-bill ratios is provided.
We are currently evaluating our overall fleet strategy and long-term plan. We are also in the process of negotiating with aircraft manufacturers and lessors to optimize timing of fleet activity.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction actions across multiple areas, including workforce management, contractual obligations, and operational expenses, with significant expected savings. However, these actions are accompanied by notable costs, such as a $250 million to $300 million expense related to early-out and voluntary furlough programs.

evidence.text: In an effort to mitigate the need for involuntary furloughs, we have initiated various early-out and voluntary furlough programs for most frontline workers and provided incentive leave options to our pilots. To date, we have had approximately 4,000 volunteers for these programs. [...] These and other cost reduction measures are critical to reaching our monthly cash burn goals. [...] The changes are expected to result in a cost of $250 million to $300 million, which will be recognized in the third quarter.
Non-fuel operating expenses, excluding special items, decreased by $467 million, or 17%, due to reduced variable costs and lower demand.

The Company has also deferred 2020 aircraft payments, including those related to the B737 MAX9, to periods beyond 2020. Discussions remain ongoing with aircraft manufacturers and lessors to optimize the timing of aircraft deliveries and lease returns.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting negatively due to reduced demand in both passenger and cargo segments, as well as the company's focus on cost-saving measures which are affecting profitability.

evidence.text: Cargo and other revenue decreased 22%, driven by reduced belly cargo activity.
The company has implemented significant cash preservation and cost reduction strategies... These measures are not expected to fully recover the loss of cash as a result of decreased ticket sales.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a significant deceleration in organic revenue growth, primarily driven by the impact of the pandemic on travel demand, which has led to a substantial decrease in total operating revenues.

evidence.text: Total operating revenues decreased $2.1 billion, or 51%, during the first six months of 2020 compared to the same period in 2019.
This reduction in demand has materially negatively impacted our revenues and results of operations.

factors.summary: The end markets are experiencing a decline due to the pandemic, particularly affecting passenger and cargo services, professional sports sponsorship costs, and marketing spend. There is a noted wariness among the public towards travel by aircraft, contributing to the unprecedented decline in both business and leisure travel.

evidence.text: End markets are weakening, particularly in passenger and cargo services, due to the pandemic.
Professional sports seasons were delayed, impacting sponsorship costs and marketing spend.
These orders, combined with a wariness among the public of travel by aircraft... have resulted in an unprecedented decline in business and leisure travel.

factors.summary: The company experienced a decrease in fuel costs due to lower prices, but faces challenges from being overhedged, which could result in cash losses. The decline in fuel expense is significant, but the hedging strategy has created a mismatch that may lead to financial strain.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel expense decreased $479 million, or 52%, compared to the six months ended June 30, 2019.
Given the sharp decline in demand and our capacity resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic, we are currently overhedged relative to our target of 50% of consumption through the remainder of 2020.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through a combination of voluntary measures and government support programs. The company has accepted conditions to use funds exclusively for employee payroll and benefits, while also benefiting from reduced wages and benefits due to voluntary leaves and reduced hours.

evidence.text: Wages and benefits decreased by $40 million, or 4%, due to voluntary leaves of absence and reduced hours.
The Company and its subsidiaries... agreed to use PSP funds exclusively for employee payroll and benefits expenses through at least September 30, 2020.
evidence.subsection: We have accepted certain conditions by accepting funding under the payroll support program of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief and Economic Security (CARES) Act.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to reduced operational needs and cost savings measures, and the company is pursuing productivity initiatives to enhance operational efficiency.

The company has implemented and continues to implement a comprehensive strategy to mitigate the impacts on our business. This strategy may itself have negative impacts on our business and operations.

evidence.text: Expectation of continued impacts to revenue throughout 2020 due to the resurgence of COVID-19 cases.

factors.summary: Management is exhibiting caution by focusing on cost preservation, liquidity enhancement, and reducing capital expenditures. This approach is also reflected in the suspension of dividends and share repurchases, indicating a risk-averse strategy.

evidence.text: Actions include securing CARES Act funding, reducing capital expenditures, and suspending dividends.
The Company has historically been able to generate sufficient cash flow from its operations to pay its debt and other fixed obligations when they become due.
